title: elymus hispidus subsp. barbulatus
intro: Elymus hispidus subsp. barbulatus, also known as rough-leaved wheat grass or barley grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and aid in digestion, due to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Elymus hispidus subsp. barbulatus include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to support overall well-being and promote a healthy lifestyle.
